title: Rath Yatra: Pulling of chariots resumes amid chants of 'Jay Jagannath'
content: Special arrangements were made to drain rainwater from the Grand Road and facilitate the procession, as devotees were pulling the chariots of Lord Jagannath

title: Jantar Mantar protest LIVE: 'Will stay alive till July 20 at any cost', says Sonam Wangchuk
content: Delhi HC to hear PIL on July 20 over 'continuous, intrusive' police surveillance at Jantar Mantar protest

title: Kerala power cuts: Pinarayi Vijayan accuses UDF govt of poor planning
content: Kerala’s Leader of the Opposition says LDF government was able to avoid such situations in the past decade by taking necessary steps in advance

title: Madras High Court omits names of TVK leaders Vijay, ‘Bussy’ Anand from writ petition on appointment of law officers
content: Justice Mohammed Shaffiq directs High Court Registry to drop their names since the petitioner’s counsel agreed not to press the case against them

title: How musicians are reinventing the centuries-old abhang
content: Through multimedia presentations, fusion bands and digital platforms, musicians are finding new ways to take the works of saint poets to contemporary audiences.

title: Kafir screenshot case in Kerala: Court rejects SIT’s plea to cancel bail of prime accused Jithin Bhaskaran
content: The prosecution’s argument is that, after being released on bail on July 3, Jithin Bhaskaran violated bail conditions by posting messages on Facebook that allegedly challenged the investigating team

title: Nagercoil Sabari Varman custodial death: Family to receive body only after experts review autopsy report
content: When Ministers S. Rajesh Kumar and Srinath had met them to hand over the financial assistance on July 16, the family refused to accept the financial assistance.

title: Supreme Court to examine whether persons deleted from voter list in SIR can be denied welfare schemes
content: Justice Bagchi noted that the Supreme Court’s Bihar SIR judgment established that the ECI cannot decide citizenship for voting rights and must refer such matters to the Ministry under the Citizenship Act

title: Dates with devotion: The division in Puri’s Rath Yatra celebrations
content: The Jagannath Temple in Puri holds its Rath Yatra every year on a date prescribed by its centuries-old ritual calendar. A branch of ISKCON, however, stages international events of the procession on dates that differ from the original, citing practical reasons. The divergence has sparked a controversy, with many in Puri viewing it as a challenge to the temple’s authority and its religious identity, finds Satyasundar Barik

title: Supreme Court to hear appeal against ban on animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath for not being true to ‘Skanda Purana’
content: In an oral mentioning, senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, representing the film’s makers, Ele Animations, said the movie was due for release on July 17 in 300-odd theatres across the country

title: SC extends Bengaluru civic polls to December due to Karnataka SIR
content: The plea had cited “severe logistical challenges” due to the ongoing Special Intensive Revision (SIR) in Karnataka

title: Kerala Waqf Board reconstitution: Pinarayi Vijayan accuses UDF govt of ‘surrendering to Sangh Parivar diktats’
content: Opposition Leader alleges that State govt has ‘betrayed' Kerala’s secular society and minorities by deciding to reconstitute Waqf Board in accordance with Union government’s Waqf (Amendment) Act

title: NEET-UG 2026 | A collection of explainers and expert opinions
content: The NEET-UG 2026 ‘paper leak’, and the structural inefficiencies that caused it, explained by The Hindu

title: NEET re-test results 2026: 12 students from Tamil Nadu score above 390
content: This year’s NEET results have indicated an increase in cut-off marks to the tune of about 30 to 35, compared to last year.

title: PM Modi flags off India’s first hydrogen-powered train from Haryana’s Jind
content: The Prime Minister also laid the foundation stone of various development projects worth around ₹14,700 crore in Jind.

title: Ahead of U.S. Ambassador arrival, Trump okays 500% tariff Bill on Russian oil; withdraws from India-led ISA
content: U.S. Ambassador-designate Sergio Gor, who takes charge on January 12, 2026 has said ending India’s Russian oil imports is a “top priority”

title: How the new U.S. visa rules affect your online privacy | Explained
content: Privacy experts have slammed the U.S. government’s new social media vetting requirements, likening the country’s approach to that of an authoritarian regime. However, they note it is difficult to claim that privacy laws are being broken as visa applicants can choose to ignore the request, though they put their visa application at risk by doing so

title: Supreme Court allows release of animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath after Rath Yatra, says animation cannot erode devotion
content: The Bench took the middle path by allowing the movie to be released, but only after the completion of the ongoing Lord Jagannath Rath Yatra on July 27, 2026. The court directed that the film could be released on or after July 28.

title: Meghalaya Assembly to move resolution against uranium mining
content: Various groups have been mounting pressure on the Conrad K. Sangma government after Minister of State for Atomic Energy Jitendra Singh said that uranium mining in Meghalaya is still under examination

title: Delhi HC to hear PIL alleging ‘intrusive police surveillance’ of Cockroach Janta Party protesters
content: The plea seeks a declaration that blanket photography, videography and operation of a surveillance tower at the protest site are unconstitutional

title: Train collides with school van in West Bengal's Murshidabad, at least three dead
content: Two of the three deceased are minors; the injured survivors have admitted to Murshidabad Medical College and Hospital

title: Delhi HC asks police to respond on bail plea by Sharjeel Imam in UAPA case
content: A Bench of Justice Prathiba M Singh and Justice Vikas Mahajan issued notice on Mr. Imam’s appeal challenging the trial court’s July 4 decision rejecting his second regular bail application; the court posted the case for hearing on August 27.

title: From ice stupas to hunger strikes: The journey of Sonam Wangchuk
content: For many Indians, Sonam Wangchuk is the real-life inspiration behind the character of Phunsukh Wangdu in 3 Idiots. But his story goes far beyond cinema. From transforming education in Ladakh and developing the world-famous Ice Stupa project to becoming the most prominent face of Ladakh’s movement for constitutional safeguards, Wangchuk’s journey has spanned education, climate action and activism. This explainer traces how an engineer and educator became one of India’s most influential public voices on Ladakh’s future.

title: Sonam Wangchuk loses over 9 kg, weakens significantly on 19th day of hunger strike: CJP
content: Sonam Wangchuk remains under constant medical supervision, the CJP said in a statement, adding that he is still ‘mentally alert’, even as he has weakened significantly

title: Madras High Court refuses to quash FIR booked against political commentator Ponraj
content: Justice G.K. Ilanthiraiyan dismisses a petition filed by him to quash the case booked for derogatory remarks against women supporters of TVK

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Why her greatest legacy lies in the Adivasi movements she helped build
content: In Mahasweta Devi’s birth centenary year, and a decade since her death, a look at the writer’s enduring impact on grassroots movements through literature and activism

title: Mahasweta Devi’s best books: Grandson Tathagata Bhattacharya recommends five must-reads
content: The writer-activist once told her grandson that she was appreciated for the wrong reasons. Recalling that conversation, he lists five works that he believes are the most powerful

title: Punjab Police officer among 37 wanted by U.S. along with gangsters Lawrence Bishnoi, Bhagwanpuria
content: The police officer is accused of working closely with Jaggu Bhagwanpuria, 38, a gangster imprisoned in India

title: Watch: Director R. Gowtham Interview by Meenakshi Shedde | Berlinale 2026
content: Debutant Tamil director R Gowtham tells Meenakshi Shedde, who has been curator/programmer of Indian/South Asian films for TIFF Toronto and the former South Asia delegate at Berlin International Film Festival, how the film ‘Members of the Problematic Family’s journey started with Diamond Rum and chicken liver. In this interview in Berlin, he describes how his film grew from his poem; how he observes life “like a dog in the balcony”; how his script, inspired by Goethe’s book, is ‘sketchy, doubtful and incomplete,’ and how he finally said ‘Action’ and ‘Cut’ only at the end of the shoot.

title: ‘Members of the Problematic Family’ movie review: R Gowtham’s explosive Tamil funeral drama
content: Tamil indie’s bold new voice R Gowtham’s debut film, which premiered in the Forum sidebar of Berlinale 2026, is an unorthodox, audacious, rare family drama

title: A Friday sermon and a day in history from 1857
content: After a Friday sermon at Mecca Masjid, Moulve Allaudin and Turrebaz Khan escaped the walled city of Hyderabad, reached the Begum Darwaza of the Residency along with 3,000 others

title: Watch: Why is Aavin reviving milk vending machines? | Focus Tamil Nadu
content: Twelve years after shutting down its automatic milk vending machines, Aavin is bringing them back. The move comes as Tamil Nadu looks to reduce plastic waste following directions from the Madras High Court. Can this decades-old system help solve a modern environmental challenge? Here are the details.

title: Give consumers option between 100% petrol and ethanol-blended petrol, Aaditya Thackeray writes to PM Modi
content: Mr. Thackeray said lakhs of vehicle owners, particularly from the middle class and youth, have expressed concerns over the rising percentage of ethanol in petrol

title: 400-year-old cannon stolen from Narwar Fort in Madhya Pradesh
content: Preliminary investigations suggest that some individuals reached the rear of the fort in a vehicle and fled with the piece of artillery kept there

title: Draft Apartment Bill gives flat owners stronger rights over common areas
content: In the proposed law, ownership of the land and common areas will remain with the flat owners collectively, and not with the association itself

title: AMMA crisis: In letter to members, Shwetha Menon says panel to ‘rectify lapses caused by lack of experience’
content: Shwetha Menon, who has reversed her decision to step down as AMMA president, says executive committee led by her is ‘duty-bound' to move forward with more responsibility

title: Mahasweta Devi at 100: Naveen Kishore remembers a life of writing as activism
content: Naveen Kishore, Mahasweta Devi’s long-time friend and poet-publisher, reflects on the phenomenon that she was and her literature of resistance

title: Akhilesh hits out at BJP over demolition notice to University linked with Azam Khan
content: The Rashtriya Janata Dal (RJD) Rajya Sabha MP Manoj Jha said the regime that seeks to destroy universities, whether with bulldozers or through administrative repression, doesn’t merely demolish buildings; it attacks thought, conscience, and the future.

title: Chembur tree-fall incident: Mumbai BMC mayor orders fresh third-party inquiry
content: The BMC mayor rejected the clean chit given to the Garden and Roads Departments, while imposing only meagre penalties to the contractors

title: Ground water levels fall in 18 districts; Telangana releases agriculture contingency plan for Vanakalam 2026
content: Majority of the major, medium, and minor irrigation reservoirs in Telangana are presently at, or very close to, their Minimum Draw Down Level (MDDL)

title: Telangana reservoirs levels constrict irrigation usage; ground water in State may plummet from 9.46 mgbl to 11.01 mgbl
content: Farmers in Telangana urged to harvest rainwater, opt less water-consuming crops. Agriculture department lists alternate crops that can be taken up in the weather conditions which changed under the impact of El Nino.

title: 16th batch of over 4600 pilgrims leave for Amarnath cave from Jammu
content: More than 3.45 lakh pilgrims have paid obeisance to Lord Shiva at the 3,880-metre-high cave shrine till date

title: Lucknow activist demands Donor Assurance Certificate from Ram Mandir Trust
content: Lucknow-based social activist Amitabh Thakur has put forth a demand to the Ram Temple Trust to ensure that the required transparency and accountability are achieved and public faith is restored

title: Two hurt as fishing boat capsizes near Visakhapatnam Fishing Harbour; all seven survive
content: The vessel overturned in strong currents and wedged between rocks near the Fishing Harbour; two injured fishermen are being treated at the KGH, less than a fortnight after six others were lost off Gangavaram

title: Provident Fund interest credited to all 34 crore accounts on July 15
content: The Union Finance Ministry had recently approved the recommendation of the EPFO’s Central Board of Trustees to provide interest at the rate of 8.25%; a senior EPFO official said the credited interest will be visible to all members when they open passbook for 2025-26.

title: BJP’s statewide protests against Omar’s J&K govt. to coincide with NC’s Jantar Mantar sit-in on Statehood
content: The BJP is preparing to organise protests over the J&K government’s outsourcing of jobs and will assemble outside the Civil Secretariat in Srinagar on July 20

title: CM Suvendu joins Rath Yatra in Kolkata; several BJP leaders join festivities
content: Suvendu Adhikari reiterates their plan to hand over mid-day meals to ISKCON, even as no official notification has been issued so far

title: Sauhardya Karnataka stages protest over NEET paper leak, expresses solidarity with Sonam Wangchuk
content: The environmentalist is on an indefinite hunger strike demanding the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET examination paper leak

title: Another hilltop muder: 2 years after wedding, Andhra woman plots husband's killing with lover's help
content: A woman and her lover allegedly murdered her husband during a family pilgrimage. The victim was lured to a remote temple road and ambushed. His wife secretly tracked their location for her accomplices. The couple's young daughter was present during the brutal attack. Police arrested the wife, her lover, and two associates.

title: PM Modi flags off India's first hydrogen train; check route, timings & how it works
content: India’s first hydrogen train is a 10-coach passenger offering. According to Indian Railways, globally the few operational or under testing hydrogen trains have 2-3 passenger coaches, making India’s first hydrogen train the longest of its type in the world.

title: E20 dispute: Maruti Suzuki to challenge order to replace car, cites fuel contamination
content: Maruti Suzuki will challenge a consumer court order directing a vehicle replacement. The court ordered the company to replace a Grand Vitara Strong Hybrid with a new E20-compatible model. The customer reported recurring technical issues after E20 petrol became widely available. Maruti Suzuki disputes the findings and claims the car was E20 compatible. The company plans to appeal the order before an appropriate higher forum.

title: First time ever: FIFA unveils special 'ring' award ahead of Messi vs Yamal final
content: The FIFA World Cup champions will receive exclusive championship rings for the first time. These rings will debut after the 2026 final between Spain and Argentina. One side features the trophy, and the other reflects the winning nation's identity. A limited collection of 2,026 rings will be available, with 30 for winners. The final match features Lionel Messi facing Lamine Yamal in a captivating generational clash.

title: Russian recruits survive just 20-30 minutes on Ukraine frontlines: CIA chief explains why
content: Russian soldiers face a brief battlefield life expectancy of minutes.  AI-powered drones have become specialized, low-cost killing machines on the front.  Ukraine's mastery of drone warfare acts as a significant equalizer in the conflict.  This technological advantage has helped Ukraine hold off Russia's superior force.  Both sides have experienced substantial casualties since the invasion began.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: MacKenzie Scott's $20m donation is Active Minds' largest ever in its history
content: MacKenzie Scott recently gifted twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant donation will expand youth mental health programs nationwide. Active Minds aims to reduce stigma and improve support access for young people. The nonprofit was founded after its leader's brother died by suicide. Scott's past financial struggles inform her philanthropic approach.

title: This billionaire flew economy and took free salt packets despite his wealth
content: IKEA founder Ingvar Kamprad lived a remarkably simple life despite his vast fortune. He consistently chose flea market clothes and economy travel, setting a personal example. Kamprad reused tea bags and ate at IKEA cafeterias, avoiding expensive dining options. His thrifty habits stemmed from his upbringing in rural southern Sweden. This frugality also shaped IKEA's company culture and employee guidelines.

title: Not Philadelphia, this lost colony may be birthplace of American ideology
content: Philadelphia's rich narrative isn't solely tied to its founding fathers' legacy; it also encompasses the short-lived Swedish colony of Nya Sverige. This settlement, which lasted seventeen years, pioneered the design of the log cabin, revolutionizing early American architecture. Furthermore, Swedish settlers introduced Lutheran Christianity and instigated critical early colonial rebellions, underscoring their significant yet brief impact in shaping America's foundations.

title: What 4,500 Google employees are demanding from CEO as tech layoffs mount
content: Nearly 100 Google employees rallied at the company's Mountain View headquarters, delivering a petition signed by 4,500 workers to CEO Sundar Pichai. Their demands: guaranteed severance, an end to GRAD performance quotas, and the option to take severance as paid leave. The Alphabet Workers Union action comes as AI-driven tech layoffs mount and Alphabet's valuation crosses $4 trillion. Here's what workers want.

title: House hunting? These tips can help you avoid AI rental scams
content: In an era of advanced technology, online rental scams have become more cunning, leveraging artificial intelligence to deceive unsuspecting tenants. Fraudsters craft fake property listings and convincingly impersonate landlords. To safeguard against these scams, it's crucial for renters to be cautious of unusually low rents and landlords who refuse inspections. Always conduct in-person visits and confirm ownership before making any financial commitments, opting for secure payment methods to mitigate risks.

title: Your landlord sold the property—can the new owner evict you?
content: When a property is sold in India, a tenant's rental agreement does not simply disappear. The new landlord is bound by the original terms set by the previous owner. Rent hikes can only occur if specified in an escalation clause within the contract. Tenants who continue paying their old landlord post-sale remain protected, and their rights to the security deposit are preserved regardless of the ownership change.

title: Gigi Hadid reportedly deepened Blake Lively's fallout with Taylor Swift
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce kept the drama away from their New York City wedding as they invited pretty much everyone from Hollywood and the NFL but Blake Lively and Ryan Reynolds, sparking chaos among many. While this has sparked a mixed reaction among fans, it seems supermodel Gigi Hadid might have made things worse for Blake Lively.

title: Who is Fátima Bosch? The Miss Universe linked to Jude Bellingham rumors
content: Miss Universe 2025 winner Fatima Bosch has attracted worldwide attention after social media users associated her with England national football team star Jude Bellingham. Bosch has denied meeting her, but the rumors have sparked a lot of interest in her life, her education, her career and her amazing journey from Mexico to becoming one of the world’s most recognizable beauty queens.

title: Marcello Hernandez's Tiger Woods joke steals the spotlight at the ESPYs
content: Marcello Hernandez delivered some of the biggest moments at the 2026 ESPYs with jokes aimed at Tiger Woods and Bill Belichick. Hernandez referenced Woods’ ongoing DUI case and poked fun at Belichick’s relationship with Jordon Hudson. The comedian’s sharp monologue drew strong reactions from the audience and became one of the night’s main talking points.

title: Falklands poster row: FIFA to probe Messi's Argentina over 'Malvinas' banner
content: FIFA is reviewing a banner displayed by Argentina players after their World Cup semi-final win. Players held a "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" message during celebrations following their victory. This gesture has led to a disciplinary review by FIFA's independent committee. Argentina's football association faced a similar fine in 2014 for the same banner. The team now prepares for the World Cup final against Spain.

title: PF for self-employed, workers in unorganised sector on way
content: Employees Provident Fund Organisation is developing a universal provident fund scheme. This new plan will cover millions in the unorganised sector and self-employed individuals. Subscribers can contribute daily or annually with tax benefits on their savings. The agency is studying global models to finalize the basic framework for this initiative. This self-financing scheme aims to extend retirement savings beyond the current EPFO network.

title: Gold price prediction: Is it time to buy gold on dips? Check outlook
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are favouring a buy on dips strategy, says Jateen Trivedi, VP Research Analyst - Commodity and Currency, LKP Securities.

title: Several trapped after landslide buries homes in China's Chongqing
content: State broadcaster CCTV said a local community worker noticed rocks falling from a hillside at around 8 am local time and issued an emergency warning. Authorities immediately began evacuating more than 60 residents, but the landslide struck at 9.08 am, trapping several people before the evacuation could be completed. Footage aired by CCTV showed a section of the hillside collapsing onto a cluster of riverside homes.

title: Uganda bus crash: At least 21 killed after school trip ends in tragedy
content: A school bus overturned in eastern Uganda, killing twenty children and the school's founder. The tragic accident occurred Thursday evening after the vehicle reportedly lost control on a steep hill. Dozens of other pupils and staff sustained injuries in the devastating crash. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about school transport safety across the nation. Uganda faces thousands of road fatalities annually due to various contributing factors.

title: Dhamaal 4' BO Day 8 [LIVE]: Ajay Devgn's comedy inches towards Rs 100 crore
content: Ajay Devgn starrer 'Dhamaal 4' has wrapped up its first week with an impressive Rs. 96 crore India net, while its worldwide gross has climbed to Rs 132.74 crore. As the film enters Day 8 and into its second weekend, all eyes are on whether the comedy entertainer can cross the coveted Rs 100 crore India net mark despite competition from Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey.'

title: India coach gives blunt 'no bridge needed' response on Kohli-Gambhir rift
content: Ahead of the first ODI at Edgbaston, Kohli was seen batting in the nets while Gambhir watched from close range without speaking to him. Kotak, meanwhile, was spotted having a conversation with the former India captain.

title: Salima Tete to lead India at Hockey WC; England, China await in pool stage
content: The squad, currently training at the national camp in Bengaluru, combines experienced players with youngsters who have impressed in recent international competitions.

title: Hegseth shares photo of Chabahar port tower collapsing amid strikes in Iran
content: Chabahar port has been a repeated target of American airstrikes. Iranian state media acknowledged a third round of strikes on the facility without immediately acknowledging the tower’s collapse. Iran, however, described the tower as overseeing commercial traffic into the port. However, Iran’s paramilitary Revolutionary Guard also operates at ports across the country.

title: 'Nothing was handed to Argentina': Messi unloads on favouritism claims
content: Argentina came from 1-0 down after the 85th minute to beat England 2-1 in Wednesday's semifinal and book a place in the final, where they will face Spain on Sunday.

title: 'If it fails, I'll return as a ghost': Wangchuk ahead of July 20 Parliament march
content: Activist Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike for the 20th day. He plans to join a march to Parliament on July 20. The Cockroach Janta Party supports Wangchuk and the ongoing protests. Political leaders have expressed concern over Wangchuk's deteriorating health. The protests demand action regarding alleged irregularities in the NEET examination.

title: 3 students killed, 4 critical after train collides with school van in Murshidabad
content: A local train struck a school vehicle at an open level crossing, killing four people. The collision occurred Friday morning between Karnasubarna station and Gobindapur rail gate. Four students and the vehicle driver were hospitalized in critical condition after the incident. Eyewitnesses alleged the rail gate remained open, leading to the accident. Investigations are underway by police and railway authorities into the cause.

title: Wife can't use RTI to access husband's ITRs for maintenance case: Delhi HC
content: Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav delivered the judgment in a writ petition filed by the husband challenging an order passed by the Central Information Commission directing disclosure of his net taxable income from the financial year 2007-08 onwards. The Central Information Commission, by its order dated 22.07.2021, had directed disclosure of the husband’s taxable income details.

title: The great Indian flip: People on roads, bikes on footpaths
content: Indian footpaths are now unsafe for pedestrians due to encroaching vehicles. Delivery riders and two-wheelers use pavements as shortcuts, creating hazards. Congestion and poor enforcement exacerbate the problem across major cities. This situation discourages walking, impacting public health and urban sustainability. Engineering, enforcement, and behavioral changes are needed to reclaim pedestrian spaces.

title: How chance discovery of Rs 40,000 in loo helped blow lid off Ayodhya Ram temple theft
content: A chance discovery of concealed cash led to an investigation into donation theft. Alerted by a gatekeeper, temple trustees initiated an internal inquiry into the matter. Police launched a probe and recovered nearly eighty lakh rupees from multiple locations. Accused individuals provided disclosures which aided further recoveries of stolen money. A special investigation team is now reconstructing the entire sequence of events.

title: Masterpiece or Overrated: 'The Odyssey' Twitter reviews divide Nolan fans
content: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' has premiered in IMAX theaters, sparking audience debate. Many fans praise its stunning visuals and Nolan's ambitious direction. However, some viewers criticize the film's pacing and storytelling choices. Dialogue and acting performances also received mixed reactions from moviegoers. Matt Damon's portrayal of Odysseus, however, earned widespread acclaim from many.

title: Wife can't be denied 'stridhan' just because she has no bills or receipts: Chhattisgarh HC
content: Chhattisgarh high court ruled a wife need not produce bills for stridhan. Husband's appeal was dismissed regarding his wife's stridhan articles. The court noted receipts are typically kept by the husband's family after marriage. Absence of documents does not negate the wife's claim for her belongings. The husband must return the stridhan or pay two lakh rupees.

title: Gujarat Anti-Terror Agency Arrests 5 Jaish Members, Foils Time Bomb Plot
content: ATS revealed that one of the arrested operatives possessed advanced knowledge of explosives and was on the verge of constructing a live time bomb.

title: 'AI Supercharging Terror Groups, Targeting Children': Australian Minister To NDTV
content: Tony Burke said the traditional architecture of terrorism- organised cells, clear ideological lines, identifiable networks- is being upended.

title: Meet Anurag Kumar, Decorated IPS Officer, Named Delhi's Police Commissioner
content: Earlier, Anurag Kumar served as a Special Director in the Intelligence Bureau (IB), the country's premier intelligence agency.

title: Kerala Heroin Network's Dada' Arrested, Lived Luxury Life On Drug Money
content: His arrest comes after police had earlier seized 609 grams of heroin worth several lakhs of rupees from three interstate migrant workers in Angamaly.

title: Gen Z Is Pushing Back Against AI: A Reminder That Future Isn't Written
content: Viral footage of grads booing AI-boosting speakers reveals a deepening generational rift, as Gen Z pushes back against tech elders' optimism

title: Anurag Kumar, Ex-Spy Agency Boss, Named Delhi's New Police Commissioner
content: Anurag Kumar, a 1994-batch IPS officer, replaced Satish Golcha, who took the charge as the Delhi Police commissioner in August last year.

title: "Will Stay Alive Till July 20": Sonam Wangchuk Refuses To Call Off Fast
content: The 59-year-old Wangchuk, who is demanding the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over the alleged NEET paper leak, has urged people to ensure a massive turnout for the Cockroach Janta Party's (CJP) proposed march to parliament on July 20.

title: 3 Things To Know About Trump's Election Fraud Allegations Againt China
content: Trump has made the claim that the 2020 elections were "rigged" more than 100 times over the first half of 2026, and has used those claims to push for Congress to pass a restrictive voter ID law called the SAVE America Act

title: Man Tries To Rape 3-Year-Old, Kills, Dumps Her In Drain After She Shouts
content: The accused, after kidnapping the child, attempted to sexually assault her, and when she raised alarm, the accused strangled her to death, police said.

title: India Gets Its 1st Hydrogen-Powered Train, Will Run On 89 Km Route In Haryana
content: The technology will use hydrogen fuel cells to produce electricity, making it a cleaner alternative to diesel trains.

title: At Least 14 Dead As Minibus Falls Into Ravine In Peru
content: A minibus carrying 19 passengers plunged into a ravine near San Juan, Peru, killing 14 and injuring five, with six bodies still unrecovered

title: 2,600 Passengers, 110 Kmph Design Speed: All About India's 1st Hydrogen Train
content: With this, India joins a select group of countries exploring hydrogen-powered rail transportation. These include Germany, Japan, China and the United States.

title: China's Xi Says AI Should Not Be Dominated By One Nation
content: AI has become a strategic pillar of China's industrial policy, driven by state investment aimed at building a domestic ecosystem, from chip production to consumer use.

title: Jagan Mohan Reddy Launched AI 'Super App' To Connect With Party Cadre
content: 'Jagan 2.0 Super App' allows party cadre to report local issues, upload complaints of alleged political harassment, access live party events, participate in campaigns, receive organisational updates, and communicate directly with the party leadership.

title: 973-Page Chargsheet Filed In Delhi IRS Officer's Daughter Rape, Murder Case
content: During interrogation, the accused Rahul Meena allegedly told investigators he went to the house "just to ask for money" and that crime "just happened."

title: UP To Tamil Nadu: Man Begins Cross-Country Foot March To Meet Vijay
content: Carrying the national flag throughout his journey, Aman said his mission is inspired by his admiration for the Chief Minister's governance, development initiatives, and welfare programmes.

title: US Immigration Officer In Maine Shooting Has History Of Violent Behavio: Report
content: David Brouillette's relatives accuse him of attacking women in his life over the years, and one shared a voicemail from last winter in which he told her that he thought someone should slit her throat.

title: Gujarat ATS widens JeM crackdown, arrests 5 more in terror module probe
content: the five accused were arrested from Khadiyal village in Siddhpur taluka . the five were produced before Judicial Magistrate R M Bhatia at Kadi in Mehsana district, who remanded them in ATS custody until July 24 . eight alleged JeM operatives were arrested on alleged module .

title: Trump will attend FIFA World Cup final, says White House; Argentina prez Milei to skip event
content: the white house confirms that president Donald Trump will join him in handing the trophy to the winners . the tournament will be held at the new york-new jersey stadium on sunday . he is expected to attend the final between argentina and spain .
